narration: That $80 prescription you just picked up? There's a four-dollar version with the exact same drug inside. Your pharmacist never told you — because nobody asked.
visual: Dark background. Two pill bottles side by side: orange brand-name bottle with $79.99 red price tag vs grey generic bottle with $3.99 green price tag. Triple-bar equals sign animates in between them. 'FDA CONFIRMED' green badge springs in below.

narration: Here's the thing — the FDA legally requires generics to have the same active ingredient, same dosage, and same absorption rate. They call it 'bioequivalent.' Same drug, different label.
visual: Light background. FDA seal SVG (green circle with FDA text). Below: orange round pill equals grey oblong pill. Three checkmark items spring in left-to-right: SAME ACTIVE INGREDIENT, SAME DOSAGE, SAME ABSORPTION RATE. 'BIOEQUIVALENT' dark badge at bottom.

narration: Generics look different on purpose — different colors, different shapes. But that's just dye and coating. Big Pharma spends six billion dollars a year making you distrust that difference.
visual: Dark background. Round orange pill vs oval white pill with a brain SVG showing question mark between them. Label: 'different dyes · different shapes · same drug.' Red dollar counter animates $0 to $6B in ad spending. 'PURE MARKETING PSYCHOLOGY' in green.

narration: This one belief costs the average American six hundred dollars every single year on drugs they didn't need to pay full price for. Nationally, that's fifty billion dollars a year.
visual: Light background. Person silhouette icon above animated red counter climbing from $0 to $600. 'PER YEAR on brand drugs' label. Divider line. '$50B' large black number reveals below with 'in unnecessary brand-name spending' and red badge.

narration: The fix is four words at the pharmacy: 'Is there a generic?' Or look it up on Cost Plus Drugs — what costs eighty dollars is often four dollars there. Same molecule.
visual: Dark background. Person silhouette at pharmacy counter with pharmacist and pill bottles on shelf. White speech bubble: 'IS THERE A GENERIC?' in green. Phone SVG showing Cost Plus Drugs screen with $80.00 struck through and $3.80 in green. 'SAME PILL · 95% CHEAPER' label.

narration: Pull out one prescription right now and check it. One thirty-second question could save you six hundred dollars a year — and the drug is literally the same molecule.
visual: Light background. Green pharmacy bag with Rx symbol. Step card: 'PULL OUT ONE PRESCRIPTION.' Arrow pointing down to green badge: 'SAVE $600/YEAR — with one 30-second question.' CTA text: 'search it at costplusdrugs.com.'
